methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Lodge Systems is a pre-seed stage company in the Defense & Security sector. The broader peer universe includes 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, with top private comparables including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries. However, the applicable comp universe for Lodge is constrained: within Defense & Security, 24 same-sector peers exist, but none satisfy the combination of same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier criteria simultaneously.

The peer-median valuation method is inapplicable due to insufficient data density. No same-stage peers with comparable profiles were identified, and only one adjacent-stage peer was located. A methodologically sound peer-median approach requires a minimum of three datapoints; the narrow screen yielded zero. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias given pre-seed stage heterogeneity and Defense & Security's distinct risk and capex profiles.

Absent triangulated comparable valuation output, no specific valuation range can be supported by the data provided. Key uncertainties limiting analysis include the scarcity of pre-seed Defense & Security transactions, the heterogeneity of the broader peer set (spanning different sub-sectors and maturity stages), and the stage-specific challenges in finding meaningful comparables. A successful valuation would require either identification of additional pre-seed or seed-stage Defense & Security peers, or deployment of alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or venture capital method analysis with explicit margin assumptions.
